text: These awards do not require an application, and many are part of funding packages given to students when they receive an admission offer. Queen’s Graduate Awards are administered by the School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs based on recommendations from individual departments. Thanks to the generosity of university benefactors, Queen’s offers numerous opportunities to graduate students to receive internal fellowships, scholarships, and awards. A list of these can be found on the Internal Awards and Financial Assistance page within the Graduate Calendar. Some awards are discipline specific.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Tri-Council is comprised of the Natural Sciences and Engineering Research Council of Canada (NSERC), the Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council (SSHRC), and the Canadian Institutes of Health Research (CIHR). Detailed information about applying can be found on each agency’s website. Students may be required to apply for one of these awards during their time at Queen’s. These prestigious awards provide significant funding for research.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Ontario Graduate Scholarships (OGS) are merit-based awards promoting excellence in Master’s and doctoral students. Eligible students must apply through the university where they will be enrolled. OGS are valued at $15,000 for one academic year, usually paid in three equal installments, and are not renewable. Students may be obligated to apply and should consult their Graduate Coordinator. Further details are available in the Academic Calendar’s Awards and Financial Assistance section.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Students have opportunities to undertake research abroad while at Queen’s. The Student Affairs website provides information about the Rhodes Scholarship program, which enables outstanding young people to study at the University of Oxford. Fulbright Canada supports exceptional Canadian students and scholars who wish to study, research, or lecture in the United States through a range of programs.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Mitacs supports fellowships and awards for Master’s students, PhD students, and postdoctoral fellows, including internship programs and student travel awards. Information on Queen’s internal application process and the Mitacs Globalink Research Award is available through University Research Services.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs Conference Travel Awards support students traveling to conferences to present their scholarship. Departments manage these applications and should be contacted directly for details.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Graduate Dean’s Travel Grant for Doctoral Field Research is a competitive award for doctoral students conducting dissertation research at a considerable distance from Queen’s. The maximum value of the award is $3,000. Graduate programs and departments receive application details approximately eight weeks prior to the annual competition deadline.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s offers various financial assistance programs, some administered by the Student Awards Office. Students may obtain needs-based financial assistance such as the Queen’s General Bursary or participate in work programs. These programs supplement, not replace, student and family contributions. The Graduate Calendar outlines additional non-competitive awards, loans, and financial assistance programs, including the Ontario Student Assistance Program (OSAP), work study, and financial assistance for students with disabilities.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Queen’s General Bursary is a non-repayable grant available to students in all years of study who demonstrate financial need. Students must be enrolled in full-time studies in a Queen’s degree program. Part-time students may apply and will be assessed individually.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Bursary for Indigenous Students is awarded based on demonstrated financial need to Indigenous students in any year of any faculty or school at Queen’s University. Applications are submitted through SOLUS to the Office of the University Registrar, Financial Services.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The SGSPA provides Maternity-Parental Leave Funding to funding-eligible doctoral students who have been granted maternity or parental leave under the Maternity and Parental Leave Regulation. The funding provides $5,000 to support a new parent taking leave from full-time graduate studies.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Ban Righ Centre offers financial assistance to women. All bursaries and awards are assessed based on the financial needs of applicants.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Society of Graduate and Professional Students (SGPS) offers Activity, Emergency Assistance, Dental, and International Student Bursaries. The SGPS Activity Bursary subsidizes participation in Queen’s University clubs, intramural teams, events, and conferences up to a maximum of $150.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Student-led initiatives organized by and directed toward graduate students may receive funding through the Dean of the SGSPA. Students must submit a Request for Funding form at least two months before funds are needed. The SGSPA Student Initiative Fund provides up to $1,000.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Vice-Provost and Dean of Student Affairs established the Student Initiative Fund to provide year-round funding for special projects and initiatives that enhance the student experience and enrich the university community. The fund supports projects promoting leadership, citizenship, intercultural awareness, community-building, and personal development. Full details and the online application form are available through the Student Affairs website.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Canada Impact+ Research Training Awards is a one-time Tri-Agency funding program designed to attract global doctoral and postdoctoral talent and strengthen Canada’s research ecosystem. The program will offer 600 doctoral awards and 400 postdoctoral awards nationally. The doctoral award is valued at $40,000 per year for three years. The postdoctoral award is valued at $70,000 per year for two years.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s University will forward top candidates as nominees in both doctoral and postdoctoral categories to the Tri-Agency. A pan-university committee will adjudicate applications to select nominees. All nomination applications for the Canada Impact+ Research Training Awards at Queen’s University must be received by Monday, February 9, 2026 at 5:00pm EST.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Canada Impact+ Research Training Doctoral Award is valued at $40,000 per year for three years and must be awarded to a student supervised by a faculty member holding active Tri-Agency funding. Faculty member nominators must meet established eligibility criteria.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Doctoral nominees may be of any citizenship, including Canadian citizens, permanent residents, and protected persons. Nominees must currently be studying or working abroad, cannot have a current affiliation with a Canadian institution, and must meet full nominee eligibility criteria.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs
